mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, generating immediate positive momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases over a 5-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the economy is in a growth phase.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trends coincide with negative SPX returns in >60% of observations during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds the 220 threshold, the Federal Reserve initiates aggressive rate hikes that compress equity multiples and increase discount rates, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: Higher nonfarm payrolls indicate robust job creation and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates immediate positive price pressure on the S&P 500 over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window or positive returns occur in <45% of high-payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.5% while personal consumption expenditures stagnate, the economy enters a demand contraction phase that reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative signal triggers positive SPX returns in >65% of instances over a 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a supply-side contraction and weakening business investment, which reduces earnings growth forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >55% of periods following two consecutive months of falling INDPRO.
